'''Tsumo''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] of a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]], and a captive of the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. Having been wanted [[Death/Legends|dead]] or alive, Tsumo was initially kept living by Fett, but when the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] encountered the [[ZZ-class freighter|''ZZ''-class freighter]] ''[[Mingula]]'' while en route to [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]], he decided to use his captive to test just how dangerous the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], which was apparently bereft of life forms, would be. Tsumo and Fett investigated the ''Mingula'', finding all manner of valuables on board, before discovering the bodies of the [[Zylurian]] crew. Seizing a moment of distraction, Tsumo attempted to kill Fett, but was himself shot by the quicker bounty hunter. The captive fell back into one of the corpses of the crew, which released a swarm of flying [[Ubuugan fleshborer|fleshborer]] [[Worm/Legends|worms]] that quickly killed Tsumo.

==Biography==
Tsumo was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] of his [[Species/Legends|species]] who in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] had a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] placed on his head, [[Death/Legends|dead]] or alive. He was subsequently apprehended by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], who elected to keep Tsumo alive for the [[Time/Legends|time]] being, holding his new captive in one of the [[Prison/Legends|holding cells]] aboard his [[Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft/Legends|''Firespray-31''-class patrol and attack craft]] [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']]. While en route to [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]],<ref name="bfs" /> the capital of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="iswu">''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''</ref> Fett picked up a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress signal]] from the [[ZZ-class freighter|''ZZ''-class freighter]] ''[[Mingula]]'', and broke from his course to investigate—the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] did not respond to any call signs, and was detected to have no life forms on board. Tsumo was retrieved from his cell by the bounty [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]], who cryptically announced that he had a task for his captive, one that could result in Tsumo's death. The captive protested, claiming that one of Fett's other [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], the [[Ubuugan]] [[Chung]], had committed worse crimes than himself. The bounty hunter was disinterested, as the Ubuugan was wanted alive and Tsumo's bounty would pay even if he were killed.<ref name="bfs" />

The pair boarded the ''Mingula'', Tsumo leading, and Fett trailing ten paces behind with a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] trained on the captive's back. Tsumo was at first unsure of his purpose, until Fett revealed that his intent was salvage. Tsumo despaired, divining that he had been brought along to ascertain how dangerous the ''Mingula'' was—any dangers would surely be triggered by him first. As they ventured further into the ship, Tsumo detected that there was indeed something wrong with the ''Mingula'' that he could not identify. Fett ordered him into the main cargo hold, which was filled to the brim with valuable items. The pair ventured upon a [[death casket]] made from highly valuable solid [[rodomium]], to Tsumo's astonishment. Fett blasted the casket open to reveal the mummified body of [[Volpau]], third cousin of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who had died of unknown causes and was being shipped to Imperial Center for ritual [[Cremation/Legends|incineration]].<ref name="bfs" />

[[File:TsumoDeath.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Tsumo is killed by the fleshborers aboard the ''Mingula''.]]
Tsumo was further perturbed by the mention of "unknown causes," but Fett nonetheless ordered him to press further into the ''Mingula'' after retrieving Volpau's valuable amulet. They soon found the bodies of all four crew members, dead and covered with what Tsumo identified as "boils." Fett, satisfied that all on board were dead, prepared to claim the ship—Tsumo however was disturbed by the sight of he dead [[Zylurian]] crew, and asked to be returned to his cell. Before the pair could take any further action however, Tsumo noticed that the boils on one of dead Zylurians were moving and shifting. As Fett moved closer to inspect, Tsumo quickly retrieved a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] from one of the fallen crew members and attempted to down Fett. The bounty hunter was too quick on the draw however, and was able to blast Tsumo in the shoulder. The captive was flung back into one of the Zylurian corpses, prompting a swarm of flying [[Ubuugan fleshborer|fleshborer]] [[Worm/Legends|worms]] to burst forth from the body's "boils." The creatures swarmed all over Tsumo, biting and burrowing into his skin. Within moments, the captive was dead from the attack. Fett, however, ultimately escaped the ''Mingula'' safely.<ref name="bfs" />

==Personality and traits==
Tsumo was a particularly fearful individual, continually predicting his own death at the hands of either Fett or the perils of the ''Mingula''. He protested Fett's treatment of him on a variety of levels, be it his usage as a test case for whatever lurked aboard the ''Mingula'', to the bounty hunter's politeness. Tsumo was continually trying to get out of his situation, pleading with Fett to release him, although the captive stopped short in one instance, citing his own inability to handle rejection. His desire to escape Fett manifested itself in an attempt to kill the bounty hunter—Tsumo however made the mistake of vocalizing his intentions, allowing the bounty hunter to get the better of him. Tsumo had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and brown skin.<ref name="bfs" />

==Equipment==
Tsumo wore a yellow-and-white [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuit]] when held captive by Boba Fett.<ref name="bfs" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tsumo first appeared in [[John Wagner]]'s [[Boba Fett ½]] ([[1997]]), penciled by [[Carlos Ezquerra]]. Although Tsumo in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] claims that Chung has committed worse acts than he, the nature of Tsumo's transgression, criminal or otherwise, is not established in the comic.
